faq.question: Is a new construction window better than a replacement window?
faq.answer: The guide “New Construction Windows vs. Replacement Windows” covers this in detail: check seal failure, drafts, condensation between panes, operation problems and rising energy bills — any two of these usually justify a professional assessment.

faq.question: Is it worth replacing 25 year old windows?
faq.answer: The guide “New Construction Windows vs. Replacement Windows” covers this in detail: check seal failure, drafts, condensation between panes, operation problems and rising energy bills — any two of these usually justify a professional assessment. Ask about New Construction Windows vs. Replacement Windows specifics when booking the free estimate.

faq.question: What is the average cost to replace 12 windows in a home?
faq.answer: Costs connected to “New Construction Windows vs. Replacement Windows” vary with material and scope: Bay Area homeowners typically see about $1,500-$3,000 per window installed for quality vinyl, and more for fiberglass, clad-wood or architectural products. A written Bay Area estimate is the only reliable way to price a specific home.
